metadata.name: Homeassistant
metadata.description: Open-source smart home automation platform that puts local control and privacy first. Supports thousands of devices and integrations.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
Open-source smart home automation platform that puts local control and privacy first. Supports thousands of devices and integrations.

## How to Use
This template renders `compose.yaml` as a file.

1. Choose the configuration path and timezone before rendering the stack.
2. Review whether you need D-Bus and optional USB device mappings for Bluetooth, Zigbee, Z-Wave, or other hardware integrations.
3. Render the Compose project, review the generated files, and start it with Docker Compose on a Docker Engine host.

## Variable Guide
### General
- `container_name`: Container Name; optional; default: homeassistant.
- `restart_policy`: Restart Policy; optional; default: unless-stopped; options: unless-stopped, always, on-failure, no.
- `service_name`: Service Name; optional; default: homeassistant.
- `container_timezone`: Container Timezone; optional.

## References
- [Home Assistant](https://www.home-assistant.io/)
- [Linux Installation](https://www.home-assistant.io/installation/linux/)
- [Documentation](https://www.home-assistant.io/docs/)

items.name: container_name
items.title: Container Name
items.description: Name to use for the container.
